content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance     Administration     Executive/Legal     Enforcement     Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660          322 5660             322 5901           322 6441          322 6444

December 14, 1979

John H. Hodgson, II
Dobbs & Nielsen
1225 Eighth St., Ste. 500
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hodgson:

I have been asked to respond to your letter of October 25,
1979, concerning the reporting requirements of Sections 84210(h)
and 84303 of the Political Reform Act of 1974.

Section 84210(h) of the California Government Code
requires candidates/committees to report in their campaign
statements:

    The full name and street address of each person
    to whom an expenditure or expenditures totaling
    one hundred dollars ($100) or more has been made,
    together with the amount of each separate expendi-
    ture to each person during the period covered by
    the campaign statement;....

In the case where an expenditure or expenditures are made to
one party for services or consideration provided by another
party, the candidate/committee is required to report:

    the full name and street address of the person
    providing the consideration for which any expendi-
    ture was made if different from the payee;....

Section 84303 of the Government Code requires candidates/
committees to report expenditures, other than normal overhead,
made on their behalf by agents or independent contractors as
if such expenditures were made directly by the candidate/
committee. There is one exception to this rule. If the
agent or independent contractor files his own campaign state-
ment, the candidate/committee does not have to provide the
identical expenditure information in its campaign statement.
This situation would occur when, for example, the agent or
independent contractor also happens to be a committee with
independent filing obligations under the Act.

John H. Hodgson, II
December 14, 1979
Page Two


     In order to ensure that the candidate or committee
receives all the expenditure information that they are
required to report, Section 84303 requires all agents and
independent contractors to "make known to the candidate or
committee all information required to be reported by this
section." This is a separate and distinct requirement
placed upon agents and independent contractors by the Act.

     The duties of a treasurer with regard to the reporting
requirements of Sections 84210(h) and 84303 are the same as
those relating to all other reporting requirements of the
Political Reform Act. A treasurer must use all reasonable
diligence in preparing and reporting the information required
to be contained in the campaign statement. A treasurer must
also verify that to the best of his or her knowledge, all
information in the campaign statement is true and complete.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Thomas M. Sobel
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

TMS:cjb

DOBBS & NIELSEN
Attorneys and Counselors at Law

SUITE 550, 1225 EIGHTH STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

(916) 446-6752


October 25, 1979


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California  95814

     Attn: Ted Prim, Executive Director

Dear Mr. Prim:

     As you are aware, this office represents a number of
political committees as well as serving as treasurer for other
committees.  Because of the recent actions by the Fair Political Practices Commission, we are writing this letter to seek
clarification of the reporting responsibilities under Government Code §84303 and §84210(h).  It is unclear what the
responsibilities of a treasurer are, particularly as they relate
to the reporting of expenditures under these two sections of the
law.  I would ask that this matter be given immediate attention
because of the confusion that exists.

     Our office would be pleased to work with your staff
to explain the current confusion as well as work towards a
clarifying regulation or opinion.

                                   Very sincerely,


                                   [signature]
                                   JOHN H. HODGSON II

JHH:laj


SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE - SUITE 2500 THE ALCOA BLDG. ONE MARITIME PLAZA SAN FRANCISCO CA 94111  •  (415) 362-1940

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting responsibilities of treasurers under California Government Code Sections 84303 and 84210(h), particularly regarding the reporting of expenditures and the disclosure requirements for agents and independent contractors?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Treasurers and agents must disclose all expenditure information required to be reported under Section 84303, with Section 84303 imposing a separate and strict requirement on agents and independent contractors to make known all required information to the candidate or committee. Treasurers must use all reasonable efforts to ensure the information reported is true and complete.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the reporting responsibilities of treasurers under California Government Code Sections 84303 and 84210(h), particularly regarding the reporting of expenditures and the disclosure requirements for agents and independent contractors?

CONCLUSION: Treasurers and agents must disclose all expenditure information required to be reported under Section 84303, with Section 84303 imposing a separate and strict requirement on agents and independent contractors to make known all required information to the candidate or committee. Treasurers must use all reasonable efforts to ensure the information reported is true and complete.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a law firm's inquiry regarding the reporting responsibilities of political committee treasurers under Sections 84303 and 84210(h) of the California Government Code, clarifying the disclosure duties of treasurers and independent contractors regarding campaign expenditures.
